channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Nintendo Switch Online April 2026 NES Haul Brings Three More Retro Classics
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo has added three new classic NES games to its Nintendo Switch Online subscription service, so you can now check out a port of a classic arcade game, a psychedelic-looking maze adventure, and an old-school dungeon crawler.

Following on from the debut of Virtual Boy titles in March, Nintendo is going back to basics with this month's additions to the Switch Online service. Unlike the Virtual Boy lineup, you won't need an Expansion Pack addon or a fancy device to play these either.

First up, it's the NES-slash-Famicom version of Pac-Man, which is one of the less disastrous home ports of the iconic arcade maze-runner. If you want to play Pac-Man on your Switch or Switch 2, this is probably the best way to experience it outside of the Arcade Archives series.

The next game to be added to Nintendo Switch Online in today's haul is Mendel Palace, the debut title from Pokemon giant Game Freak.

This one is a panel-flipping puzzler complete with a colorful, psychedelic aesthetic. Don't expect the sophistication of Game Freak's later Pokemon RPGs, but this is still a neat little slice of history, so check it out if you want to see where the studio came from.

The final new game in today's Switch Online NES lineup is dungeon-crawler The Tower of Druaga, which is sure to appeal to fans of games like Gauntlet. Essentially, it's about traversing mazes, picking up items to buff yourself, and defeating nefarious foes in order to reach the top floor of the titular tower.

You can check out a trailer of all three games in action right here.

Pac-Man, Mendel Palace, and The Tower of Druaga are all available right now for Nintendo Switch Online subscribers. As a reminder, you'll only need the base version of the service if you want to take a look at them (along with any other NES games you're interested in).

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/nintendo-switch-online-april-2026-nes
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Home Safety Hotline Movie Confirmed by Developer
item.content:encoded:
Nick Lives, the creative director behind Home Safety Hotline developer Night Signal Entertainment, has confirmed that the spooky telephone operator sim is getting itself a movie adaptation.

We don't know too much about the movie right now, but news of its existence was first reported by the folks at Bloody Disgusting before being confirmed by Lives himself on Bluesky.

According to Bloody Disgusting's report, Home Safety Hotline's movie adaptation will be directed by Love and Monsters helmsman Michael Matthews, and scriptwriting duties will be handled by Nick Tassoni. It looks as though the project will serve as Tassoni's feature-length debut in the industry.

Producer Steven Schneider says his production company Spooky Pictures is well-suited to adapting Home Safety Hotline; he says adapting "beloved IP into visceral cinematic experiences" is what the company does best.

Executive producer Ben Ross, meanwhile, points to Apple TV hit Severance and iconic Netflix show Stranger Things as tonal touchpoints for the movie, describing the project as "analog horror at its spine-chilling best".

It's difficult to imagine how Home Safety Hotline could be turned into a movie without losing a lot of its identity; the game is largely a Hypnospace Outlaw-style operating system sim, with the calls you'll field as a player taking place inside that environment.

Still, the game is far from the only eyebrow-raising adaptation to be announced in recent months. In February, Netflix scored the rights to making movies and TV shows based on board games Ticket to Ride and Catan, and games like Phasmophobia and The Mortuary Assistant have also been earmarked for movie adaptations.

As for Home Safety Hotline itself, you can check it out right now on PC via Steam if you want to get some revision in before a release date for the movie lands. It's a wild ride, so I'd recommend doing so if you're an analog horror fan.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/home-safety-hotline-movie
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Mortal Kombat 1 Sales Pass 8m, Netherrealm Confirms
item.content:encoded:
Developer Netherrealm and publisher Warner Bros have revealed that their krossover-laden fighting game Mortal Kombat 1 has reached a brand new sales milestone just over two and a half years after its initial release.

In a post on X, the two studios announce that Mortal Kombat 1 has now reached "8 million units sold and kounting", thanking the game's "kommunity" for its ongoing support.

As of January last year, the game had managed to sell five million kopies, so that means in the intervening year and change, Mortal Kombat 1 has racked up another three million sales. Given the way things are going for Warner Bros right now, though, I'm not sure how much that means for the bigger picture.

Mortal Kombat 1 was first released back in September 2023. It's technically the twelfth mainline game in the series, although since its story effects another reboot, you kan (sort of) play this one without knowing anything about previous titles.

The game packs in iconic characters from previous titles, with returning fighters including the likes of Baraka, Reptile, and Johnny Cage, although some of them may look a little different to the way you remember.

After launch, the usual slew of krossover DLC for Mortal Kombat 1 was announced. The first DLC fighter, Omni-Man from Invincible, was released in November 2023, and subsequent additions to the game included the likes of Peacemaker's eponymous antihero, The Boys' Homelander, and the T-1000.

Mortal Kombat 1 received mostly positive reviews across the board, although not for the Switch version, which was singled out for its atrocious performance and lengthy loading times. Unfortunately, no Switch 2 release has been announced yet.

You can check out Mortal Kombat 1 right now on PC, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Nintendo Switch (although I'd recommend playing the game on one of the former three platforms if you can).

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/mortal-kombat-1-sales-8m
